title: ArtifactPointer
description: A journal-relative pointer (path + content digest) to a stage artifact. The only way stages exchange non-scalar data (ARCHITECTURE.md §5).

path.description: Journal-relative path to the artifact bytes. Must be contained: not absolute (no leading '/') and no '..' path component. This pattern is a conservative superset of the Go containment check (ArtifactPointer.Resolve / ResolveContainedPath): it rejects every '..' segment — including the contained '..' that filepath.Clean collapses and ResolveContainedPath accepts (e.g. 'a/../b') — so it is stricter than, not equal to, the resolve-time check. That means a foreign-authored envelope that would escape the journal fails at validate time, not only at resolve time, and the divergence is always in the safe direction: nothing the schema accepts is rejected at resolve time (#125). Uses an RE2-compatible pattern (no lookahead): each '/'-separated segment must contain a non-dot char, or be a lone '.', or be three-or-more dots — so a '..' segment can never appear.
